most_recent_game.recap_text:
HAMMOND, La. -
Behind a pair of strong pitching efforts, early offense and a go-ahead run in the eighth inning, Stephen F. Austin staved off a Southland Conference sweep by earning a 3-2 victory over Southeastern Louisiana in the series finale from Pat Kenelly Diamond at Alumni Field. The win moves SFA to 12-9 overall with a 5-4 record in SLC play. SLU drops to 11-9 and 4-2 in conference action.
A groundout by
Mark Henning
plated the game's first run as
Cooper Schneider
scored for an early 1-0 lead to back starter
Dylan Mulcahy
. The Jacks pushed a second tally across in the second inning on a second RBI groundout, this time from
Cermodrick Bland
, that plated
Juan Cantua
.
The Lions responded in the home half of the second inning with a pair of runs, one via groundout and the second on a single. Three of the Lions' eight hits were produced in the second inning. Mulcahy pitched a perfect third, tossed around a lead off single to third base in the fourth and worked around a one-out single to first base in the fifth as his five-inning effort resulted in six strikeouts, no walks and six hits with just one for extra bases.
Nate Vidlak
took over for Mulcahy in the sixth inning and finished out the final four innings allowing just two hits and striking out four of the final seven batters he faced. In the eighth, the Jacks grabbed the lead as a single by Cantua moved runners, while a fielding error in left centerfield allowed Ethan Peña to advance an extra 90 and score from first base.
The lead was upheld as a two-out single in the SLU half of the ninth was left stranded as Vidlak punched out his seventh batter to pick up the win.
POSTGAME NOTES
Peña's double in the fifth inning was the 40th for the Jacks this season and the lone extra base hit in the win
Peña (11) and Cantua (7) each recorded two-hit games
Cantua moved his team-leading active reached safely streak to ten games to become the third Jack (Bland, Acreman) to produce a streak of 10+ games this season
Schneider, from his lead off spot, drew three of SFA's five walks
Jhett Creel
extended his team-leading active hit streak to five games
Mulcahy pushed his season K total to 22 as his six strikeouts and five innings pitched were both single-game highs
Vidlak's seven punch outs, his second appearance with seven Ks, makes him the second Jack to reach two dozen strikeouts this season
SFA stranded eight SLU base runners and walked just one Lion
SECURE YOUR SEATS TODAY
Single game tickets
for all SFA baseball home games are available now. Fans interested in purchasing season ticket packages can secure their seats
here
. Don't miss out on all the exciting action from Pilgrim's Park this season!
THE JACKS HAVE GONE MOBILE
SFA Athletics' newest mobile phone application is designed to bring Lumberjack fans closer to the action than ever before. Available now on both the App Store and Google Play, the new SFA Athletics app delivers schedules, scores, rosters, news, ticket access, and exclusive content right to your phone. Fans can also receive push notifications, so they never miss a moment of the action. Download now:
Official SFA Athletics App
.
FOLLOW THE JACKS
For all the latest information on SFA baseball, visit
SFAjacks.com
. For up-to-the-minute updates, follow the Jacks on
X
,
Instagram
and
Facebook
.
